description: Among Georges Braque’s largest and most ambitious still lifes of the 1930s, this work features complex ornamentation and playfully overlapping patterns that create rhythmic movement. An example of what he called “rhyming shapes” and “metamorphic confusion,” the round fruits on the table mimic the holes of stringed instruments, and the curving lines of the mandolin evoke a fruit bowl.
title: Still Life with Fruits and Stringed Instrument
